sections.title: Introduction to Imperative
sections.content: The imperative mood is used to give commands, instructions, or orders. It corresponds to the imperative in English (e.g., Go! Speak! Listen!). In French, the imperative exists for the tu, nous, and vous forms.

sections.title: Formation for Regular Verbs
sections.content: For regular verbs, the imperative is formed from the present tense conjugation. For -ER verbs, the tu form drops the -s. The nous and vous forms are the same as the present tense.
examples: Parler: parle! (speak! - tu), parlons! (let's speak! - nous), parlez! (speak! - vous)
Finir: finis! (finish! - tu), finissons! (let's finish! - nous), finissez! (finish! - vous)
Vendre: vends! (sell! - tu), vendons! (let's sell! - nous), vendez! (sell! - vous)

sections.title: Formation for Irregular Verbs
sections.content: Irregular verbs have irregular imperative forms. The most common are être, avoir, aller, and faire.
examples: Être: sois! (be! - tu), soyons! (let's be! - nous), soyez! (be! - vous)
Avoir: aie! (have! - tu), ayons! (let's have! - nous), ayez! (have! - vous)
Aller: va! (go! - tu), allons! (let's go! - nous), allez! (go! - vous)
Faire: fais! (do/make! - tu), faisons! (let's do/make! - nous), faites! (do/make! - vous)

sections.title: Affirmative vs Negative Imperative
sections.content: In affirmative commands, the object pronouns come AFTER the verb and are connected with hyphens. In negative commands, the object pronouns come BEFORE the verb, like in regular sentences.
examples: Negative: Ne le regarde pas! (Don't look at it!)
Negative: Ne m'écoute pas! (Don't listen to me!)
Negative: Ne la mangeons pas! (Let's not eat it!)

sections.title: Imperative with Reflexive Verbs
sections.content: With reflexive verbs, the reflexive pronoun comes after the verb in affirmative commands, but before the verb in negative commands.
examples: Negative: Ne te lève pas! (Don't get up!)
Negative: Ne nous asseyons pas! (Let's not sit down!)
Negative: Ne vous habillez pas! (Don't get dressed!)

sections.title: Common Imperative Expressions
sections.content: Many common phrases use the imperative mood.

sections.title: Polite Imperative with Vous
sections.content: The vous form of the imperative is used for formal commands or when addressing multiple people. It's the same as the present tense vous form.
examples: Asseyez-vous, s'il vous plaît! (Please sit down!)
Veuillez entrer (Please come in) - very formal

sections.title: Practice Exercise
sections.content: Write 10 commands using the imperative mood: 1) Tell someone to speak. 2) Tell someone to eat. 3) Tell a group to listen. 4) Tell someone not to go. 5) Tell someone to sit down. 6) Tell a group to be quiet. 7) Tell someone to wait. 8) Tell someone to come here.
